repo.name: helix-editor/helix
repo.scope: the Helix modal text editor (Rust, multi-crate Cargo workspace)

reference_claims.text: helix-core is a crate: the core editing primitives.
helix-view is a crate: UI abstractions / the imperative shell.
helix-term is a crate: the terminal UI (the main editor binary frontend).
helix-lsp is a crate: the Language Server Protocol client.
helix-dap is a crate: the Debug Adapter Protocol (DAP) client.
helix-event is a crate: primitives for defining and handling events within the editor.
helix-loader is a crate: functions for building, fetching, and loading external resources.
helix-tui is a crate: TUI primitives.
helix-stdx is a crate: extensions to the standard library.
helix-lsp-types is a crate: Language Server Protocol type definitions.
helix-core contains the basic building blocks used to construct the editor (core editing primitives).
helix-view is the imperative shell built on top of core that provides common editor logic; Document, View and Editor live here.
helix-term is the terminal frontend.
helix-lsp is a Language Server Protocol client.
helix-dap is a Debug Adapter Protocol client.
helix-loader provides functions for building, fetching, and loading external resources (e.g. grammars/config).
helix-view depends on helix-core (the view layer builds on top of core).
helix-term depends on helix-view (the terminal frontend builds on the view layer).
The layering is core -> view -> term: helix-core does not depend on helix-view or helix-term (core is the innermost functional layer).
Core primitives are functional: most operations do not modify data in place but return a new copy.
The main data structure for representing buffers is a Rope (the ropey library, re-exported); ropes are cheap to clone, enabling snapshots of text state.
A Selection is the multiple-selection primitive: it consists of Ranges, each with a moving head and an immovable anchor; a single cursor is a selection with one range whose head and anchor coincide.
Ropes are modified by constructing an OT-like Transaction that represents one coherent change, can be applied to the rope, and can be inverted to produce an undo.
A Document ties together the Rope, Selection(s), Syntax, History and language server into a representation of an open file.
Multiple Views can display the same Document, so the document stores selections per view (document.selection() takes a ViewId).
The Editor holds the global state: all open documents, a tree of view splits, the configuration, and a registry of language servers.
Syntax highlighting and AST interaction use tree-sitter (the Syntax type is the interface to tree-sitter ASTs).
helix-tui is forked from tui-rs (inspired by Cursive).
The Rope implementation used is the ropey library (re-exported by helix-core).
Core's design is heavily based on CodeMirror 6.
helix-term's main function sets up a new Application that runs the editor event loop.

emitted_claims.source_region: AGENTS.md component registry row + architecture.md overview
emitted_claims.text: helix is a single Cargo workspace (resolver "2") of 14 library/runtime crates plus a dev-only xtask tooling member.
helix-term is the top-of-stack binary crate producing the `hx` executable; it owns the async event loop, the TUI compositor, the keymap/command surface, and LSP/DAP/job plumbing.
helix-view is the mid-layer editor-state + UI-abstraction crate: it owns Editor, Document buffers, View windows, the Tree split-layout, and Theme.
helix-core is the core editing-primitives library: the Rope text model, Selection, the invertible Transaction algebra, and Syntax/Movement/Indent.
helix-tui is the terminal-UI rendering library: a double-buffered grid of styled Cells, diffed and emitted to a pluggable Backend.
helix-lsp is the LSP client: it spawns each language server as a child process and drives JSON-RPC 2.0 over its stdio; it exposes Client + Registry.
helix-lsp-types is a pure serde data-model crate for VS Code's LSP messages, one file per protocol feature, re-exported flat.
helix-dap is the Debug-Adapter-Protocol client: it frames DAP JSON-RPC over stdio or TCP; it exposes Client + Registry.
helix-dap-types is a pure, dependency-free serde data-model crate for the Debug Adapter Protocol wire types.
helix-event is a dependency-free foundation crate: a decoupled (often async) hook/event system plus redraw/status queues.
helix-loader is the build/runtime bootstrapping crate: it resolves filesystem dirs, merges layered TOML configs, fetches/compiles tree-sitter grammars, and manages workspace-trust.
helix-vcs is the VCS integration crate: a file's committed base version, git-status, and a live debounced background line-diff for the change gutter (git via gix).
helix-stdx is the foundation standard-library-extensions leaf crate: env/cwd/which/path/range/uri/faccess/rope helpers.
helix-parsec is a zero-dependency parser-combinator library: a Parser trait plus composable combinators.
xtask is the developer-tooling/automation binary (the cargo-xtask pattern): docgen / query-check / indent-check / theme-check.
helix-term depends on helix-view (import; Editor as the central owned state).
helix-term depends on helix-core (import; Rope/Selection/Transaction/Syntax).
helix-term depends on helix-lsp (import + runtime-call; routes LanguageServerMessage).
helix-term depends on helix-dap (import + runtime-call).
helix-term depends on helix-vcs (import; FileChange/Hunk, behind the git default feature).
helix-term depends on helix-loader (import + runtime-call; config/log/runtime dirs, grammar, workspace_trust).
helix-term depends on helix-stdx (import; Url/path/env/rope).
helix-term depends on helix-event (import + runtime-call; events!/start_frame/request_redraw/AsyncHook/status).
helix-term depends on helix-tui (project-ref, package `tui`: Backend/Terminal/Buffer/widgets).
helix-view depends on helix-core (path-dep import).
helix-view depends on helix-event (path-dep; queue-event/dispatch).
helix-view depends on helix-loader (path-dep; runtime-call workspace-trust).
helix-view depends on helix-lsp (path-dep; import + runtime-registry).
helix-view depends on helix-dap (path-dep; import + runtime-registry).
helix-view depends on helix-vcs (path-dep; diff handle/registry).
helix-view depends on helix-stdx (path-dep; path/file/env primitives).
helix-view depends on helix-tui as a dev-dependency ONLY (doctests); it is NOT a runtime edge.
helix-core depends on helix-stdx (project-ref + import; RopeSliceExt).
helix-core depends on helix-loader (project-ref + import; grammar::get_language, config::default_lang_config).
helix-core depends on helix-parsec (project-ref + import).
helix-tui depends on helix-view (project-ref path-dep, term feature).
helix-tui depends on helix-core (project-ref path-dep).
helix-lsp depends on helix-lsp-types (import; path-dep, re-exported as `lsp`).
helix-lsp depends on helix-core (import; Rope/ChangeSet/Transaction/diagnostic/diff).
helix-lsp depends on helix-loader (import; find_workspace, VERSION_AND_GIT_HASH).
helix-lsp depends on helix-stdx (import; path::normalize, env::which/cwd).
helix-lsp-types depends on helix-stdx (import + project-ref; `pub use helix_stdx::Url`) and on no other helix crate.
helix-dap depends on helix-dap-types (import; path-dep, wholesale re-export).
helix-dap depends on helix-core (import; DebugAdapterConfig/DebuggerQuirks from helix_core::syntax::config).
helix-dap depends on helix-stdx (import; env::which + ExecutableNotFoundError).
helix-loader depends on helix-stdx (project-ref + import + runtime-call env::which("git")); it is the only intra-workspace dependency.
helix-vcs depends on helix-core (project-ref + import).
helix-vcs depends on helix-event (project-ref + runtime-call).
helix-dap-types, helix-event, helix-stdx, and helix-parsec are leaf crates with no internal (intra-workspace) outgoing edges.
xtask depends on helix-core, helix-term, helix-view, and helix-stdx (imports).

emitted_claims.source_region: AGENTS.md technical dependencies + critical-facts.md non-obvious + xtask.md
emitted_claims.text: xtask declares helix-loader (and the external `toml` crate) as manifest dependencies but references NEITHER anywhere in xtask/src/ ג€” manifest-only edges (manifest-vs-code drift).

emitted_claims.source_region: AGENTS.md architecture + technical-dependencies.md helix-core layering note
emitted_claims.text: helix-core is foundation-only: it may depend only on helix-stdx/helix-loader/helix-parsec and on no higher crate; a grep for use of view/term/tui/lsp/dap/event/vcs in helix-core/src returned no matches.

LAYERING DIRECTION: helix-term sits on top and depends on every other runtime crate; they do NOT import it. helix-view is a consumer of (not a dependency to) helix-term ג€” no helix_term reference appears in helix-view/src.
The crate split is enforced by Cargo's manifest DAG (Cargo bans cross-crate dependency cycles), forming an acyclic graph that fans in toward foundation leaves.

emitted_claims.source_region: AGENTS.md Critical-to-know entry-point bullet + critical-facts.md startup chain + helix-term.md
emitted_claims.text: System entry point and ordered boot chain: main -> main_impl (#[tokio::main]) -> Args::parse_args -> helix_loader::initialize_config_file/log_file -> early-exit flags -> setup_logging -> set CWD -> Config::load_default -> user_lang_loader -> Application::new -> app.event_stream() -> app.run() -> process::exit.
run() lifecycle (ordered): terminal.claim() -> event_loop -> close() (drain jobs -> flush_writes -> close_language_servers) -> restore_term() -> propagate close errors into exit_code.

The main event loop is a biased tokio::select! (biased;): signals first, then terminal input, job callbacks, status messages, wait-futures, editor events; the loop terminates when editor.should_close().

Application::new construction order: theme loader -> backend -> Terminal -> Compositor -> ArcSwap<Config> -> handlers::setup -> Editor::new -> load theme -> push EditorView layer -> Jobs::new -> open files/tutor/stdin -> build signal stream.

emitted_claims.source_region: AGENTS.md Critical-to-know data structure + critical-facts.md + helix-core.md
emitted_claims.text: CORE DATA STRUCTURE: the buffer is a ropey::Rope re-exported as the canonical text type (with a SmartString-backed Tendril for small edit strings); cheap clones/snapshots enable value-semantics editing.

Edit algebra has value semantics and is composable + invertible: Transaction/ChangeSet describe docA->docB; compose combines docA->docB with docB->docC; invert(original) produces the reverting transaction; a ChangeSet len guard refuses to apply unless lengths match.
A Selection is never empty (always contains at least the primary range); new() asserts !ranges.is_empty() and remove() refuses the last range.
Document::apply_impl mutates self.text in place, increments self.version, and re-maps EVERY view's selection + view_position so all views stay consistent.
DocumentId is never zero: it wraps NonZeroUsize so Option<DocumentId> is one byte; the default id is 1.
Partial-borrow rule: cross-field access on Editor MUST go through the current!/doc_mut!/view_mut! macros (not &mut self methods) so Rust sees a partial borrow rather than the whole struct.

Config mutation is single-owner: only the Application applies config changes; the Editor sends an updated copy up via ConfigEvent::Update. Config is shared lock-free via arc_swap::ArcSwap and projected with arc_swap::access::Map into sub-views.

Error-propagation / no-crash-after-startup: helix-view/core/lsp/dap/loader/tui/vcs return anyhow::Result / typed errors / io::Result rather than crashing; the term shutdown path does not return early ג€” it runs all cleanup and collects errors into a Vec<anyhow::Error>.

Compositor ordering: render order is bottom-to-top (later-pushed layers draw on top); event propagation is top-to-bottom bubbling through layers .rev(), stopping at the first Consumed; cursor resolution: front-most layer wins.
Editor::wait_event is the single tokio::select! source of truth, biased so document-saves win, then config/LSP/DAP, then redraw vs idle timers.
Ordered LSP shutdown: close_language_servers first removes every client from the file-event handler (non-blocking), THEN force-shutdowns all clients under a timeout (default 3000ms).

emitted_claims.source_region: AGENTS.md Critical-to-know tech choice + critical-facts.md + helix-term.md
emitted_claims.text: Terminal backend is compile-time selected: Termina (non-Windows), Crossterm (Windows), TestBackend under the integration feature.
Binary-file detection is a hand-rolled is_binary heuristic (BOM check, then NUL-in-first-1KB or PDF/PNG magic), explicitly replacing the content_inspector crate.
helix-lsp-types, helix-dap-types, helix-parsec and xtask are NOT direct manifest dependencies of helix-term; the LSP/DAP types are reached transitively via helix_lsp::lsp / helix_dap re-exports.
Document buffer store: Editor.documents is a BTreeMap<DocumentId, Document> keyed by a NonZeroUsize id; document text is a persistent Rope plus per-view selections/view_data maps.
Breakpoints store: Editor.breakpoints is a HashMap<PathBuf, Vec<Breakpoint>> (in-memory, per file path).
Registers (yank store): Registers.inner is a HashMap<char, Vec<String>>, with special registers backed by the system clipboard.
TUI double frame buffer: Terminal.buffers is a [Buffer; 2] with a current index (no disk/DB persistence); a thread-local LAYOUT_CACHE: RefCell<HashMap<(Rect, Layout), Vec<Rect>>> memoizes layout.

LSP client registry: a SlotMap<LanguageServerId, Arc<Client>> plus a parallel HashMap<LanguageServerName, Vec<Arc<Client>>>; the transport holds an in-flight request table Mutex<HashMap<jsonrpc::Id, Sender<Result<Value>>>>.

DAP client registry: a SlotMap<DebugAdapterId, Client> plus a merged SelectAll incoming stream; per-client live debug state (stack_frames/thread_states/progress/caps); a transport correlation table pending_requests Mutex<HashMap<u64, Sender<Result<Response>>>> keyed by request seq.
VCS diff state: shared DiffInner{diff_base: Rope, doc: Rope, hunks: Vec<Hunk>} held behind Arc<RwLock<DiffInner>>.
Global event registry: events map + handlers map behind an RwLock (a process global); a global status-message mpsc queue (capacity 128); global redraw signals (a Notify + RwLock<()>).
Process-global cwd cache: static CWD: RwLock<Option<PathBuf>> in helix-stdx, kept static so it is reachable without the Editor.
Runtime-dir chain: RUNTIME_DIRS, a process-wide lazy Vec<PathBuf> priority chain in helix-loader.

There is no database / ORM / network-backed persistence layer; helix's data layer is in-memory editor state plus on-disk file I/O for documents, config, themes, grammars, logs, and workspace-trust lists.

Document files: Document::open reads via std::fs::File::open; Document::save_impl writes via tokio::fs honoring atomic_save; a save snapshots self.text().clone() into a 'static+Send future; a non-forced save refuses to clobber a file modified on disk since last save.
Themes on disk: theme::Loader searches the themes/ subdir of each runtime dir and read_to_strings <name>.toml.

Config files: global config.toml + per-workspace .helix/config.toml are read and trust-gated merged; the global languages.toml plus a built-in languages.toml embedded at compile time via include_bytes!.
Workspace-trust lists: trusted_workspaces + excluded_workspaces files under the data dir (newline-delimited path lists, read + write).

Tree-sitter grammars: vendored git checkouts in runtime/grammars/sources/<g> and compiled dylibs in runtime/grammars/<name>.{so,dll,dylib,wasm}; query files (highlights/injections/locals .scm) read by helix-core via helix-loader.
Language servers are each spawned as a child process; the client drives JSON-RPC over its stdio.
Debug adapters are each a spawned child process (stdin/stdout/stderr) or a TcpStream.
System clipboard is accessed via an external process / OS API (Windows uses clipboard-win).

emitted_claims.source_region: technical-dependencies.md key external dependencies + critical-facts.md tech choices
emitted_claims.text: Language/build: Rust 2021 edition, workspace pinned to rust-version 1.90; Cargo workspace resolver "2" with 15 members; single binary hx from helix-term.
Async runtime: Tokio multi-thread, entry via #[tokio::main]; async glue via futures-util / futures-executor / tokio-stream.
Text storage: ropey rope data structure with the SIMD feature.
Syntax: tree-house / tree-sitter grammars fetched + built by helix-loader.

emitted_claims.source_region: technical-dependencies.md key external dependencies + critical-facts.md + helix-tui.md
emitted_claims.text: TUI layout is solved with the cassowary constraint solver (REQUIRED constraints for bounds, WEAK for sizing).

emitted_claims.source_region: technical-dependencies.md key external dependencies + critical-facts.md + helix-lsp.md
emitted_claims.text: LSP transport: JSON-RPC over tokio using sonic-rs (incoming) / serde_json (outgoing); OffsetEncoding is negotiated, default UTF-16.
Version control: gix (gitoxide) behind the optional git feature; diffing uses imara-diff (Algorithm::Histogram + a 4-space IndentHeuristic).
Fuzzy matching / picker uses nucleo plus ignore/grep-*.
Concurrency primitives: parking_lot locks + arc-swap; the event-registry RwLock uses hardware-lock-elision so it is essentially free under the read-mostly pattern.
unicode-width is deliberately version-pinned to =0.1.12 to avoid width-definition changes that disagree with terminals.

History is a single-rooted append-only revision tree (Vec<Revision> + current index) that always has >=1 element (the empty root), storing BOTH a transaction and its inversion per revision; undo applies the inversion, redo applies the last-child transaction; the revision vector is unbounded.

TUI buffer length invariant: Buffer.content.len() is always area.width * area.height, preserved by resize/merge; double-buffering draw flushes the diff, resets the back buffer, then flips current = 1 - current.

LSP wire-faithfulness invariant: the serde round-trip is the core invariant the helix-lsp-types crate exists to guarantee (deserialize(serialize(x)) == x); optional fields are omitted not null; Position/Range are zero-based and Range.end is EXCLUSIVE.

DAP seq monotonicity: request ids come from an AtomicU64 via fetch_add+1 so the first message is 1 and each is exactly 1 greater; Module.id is coerced to String on deserialization even when the adapter sends a JSON number.
helix-event cancellation state is packed into ONE AtomicU64 ג€” low 32 bits = generation counter, high 32 bits = num_running refcount; cancelling bumps the generation.

emitted_claims.source_region: critical-facts.md boundary rules security gates + helix-loader.md + helix-view.md
emitted_claims.text: Security gate: opening files consults helix-loader workspace-trust before trusting workspace config; an untrusted workspace's .helix/languages.toml must NOT be merged (user_lang_config adds the workspace file only when trust == Trusted); insecure==true short-circuits to Trusted/AllowAlways.

Widget / backend isolation: widgets must NOT touch the terminal directly ג€” they render only into an intermediate Buffer; all terminal I/O is funneled through the Backend trait and Terminal<B> never calls an OS API directly.

LSP transport isolation: Client never touches child stdio; it sends Payload over a channel to Transport, whose three dedicated tokio tasks (recv/err/send) are the sole readers/writers of the process pipes.

helix-event decoupling rule: components communicate via events/hooks instead of importing each other directly; events must be declared via the events! macro and hooks via register_hook!, not by hand-implementing the trait.
LSP registry tombstones: stop() drains a server's Vec<Arc<Client>> but leaves the empty entry in inner_by_name as a tombstone so get() will NOT auto-restart a manually-stopped server.
DAP two-transport reaping asymmetry: stdio spawns the child with kill_on_drop(true) (reaped), while tcp_process spawns detached and is NOT killed on leave (sleeping 500ms before connect).

helix-stdx uses a custom RFC3986 Url (a verbatim-String newtype parsed lazily) instead of the url crate, because LSP uses RFC3986 (not WHATWG) percent-encoding and strict servers reject unescaped [ ].
helix-event hashing is non-DOS-resistant by design: HashMaps use foldhash with a hardcoded fixed seed; DOS resistance is explicitly a non-goal.

xtask indent-check gating asymmetry: a reindent mismatch always counts as an error; in the typing simulation, UNDER-indent is always a failure but OVER-indent fails only when the next line's leading token is NOT an @outdent token ג€” the check gates on under-indents only.
All positioning in helix-core is char-offset based at this boundary: positioning is done via char offsets into the buffer; Range anchor/head are gap indices between chars.

Syntax is built on the tree-house tree-sitter wrapper; Language/Syntax are re-exported from helix-core; LanguageData lazily compiles per-language queries via OnceCell (compute-once caching) and a missing grammar shared library degrades gracefully to Ok(None).
The language Loader indexes languages by extension/shebang/glob for file-type detection (languages_by_extension/languages_by_shebang HashMaps + a FileTypeGlobMatcher).
helix-core reads on-disk .editorconfig files for a given path by walking ancestor directories (read-only).
Window layout: Tree is a SlotMap<ViewId, Node> of View/Container nodes with a root, a focus, and a recomputed area; geometry is recomputed on resize/tree-change.
Lazy view sync: cross-document edits are synced lazily on window switch; View.doc_revisions records the last applied history revision per doc so only new changes replay.

In-process event bus: editor lifecycle events (DocumentDidOpen/Change/Close, SelectionDidChange, ConfigDidChange, LanguageServerInitialized/Exited) are declared via the helix_event::events! macro and fired with dispatch.
Minimal-update invariant: Buffer::diff returns only the changed cells as (x, y, &Cell), skipping cells hidden behind preceding multi-width graphemes.
helix-tui default terminal size is 80x24 whenever the backend cannot report a size.
Robustness: a grapheme exceeding SYMBOL_CAPACITY = 28 bytes is replaced by U+FFFD rather than truncated/panicking; widths are clamped into a u8.
LSP capabilities are initialized-once (capabilities: OnceCell); capabilities()/supports_feature PANIC if called before init.
Initialize gate / message ordering: the LSP send task queues every non-initialize request until the server replies, then drains the queue in order to keep request order consistent.

Overlapping/out-of-order LSP edits are made safe before building a ChangeSet: edits are sorted, a full-document replacement becomes a rope diff, and any overlapping or unmappable edit is dropped (not panicked).
helix-lsp-types forbids unsafe code crate-wide (#![forbid(unsafe_code)]) and gates unstable/proposed LSP extensions behind a `proposed` Cargo feature (compiled out by default, no semver guarantee).

LSP integer enums are newtype-over-i32 with an open-ended fallback (via a lsp_enum! macro), not Rust enums, so an unknown numeric value round-trips instead of failing deserialization (forward-compat with newer servers).

DAP request/response correlation: a request carries a one-shot back_ch reply channel (#[serde(skip)]); the transport registers it in pending_requests keyed by seq and routes the matching Response back; a response to an unknown seq is forwarded with a warning, never dropped.
DAP request timeout is a hard-coded 20s per call.

DAP initialize advertises fixed client capabilities (client_id "hx", name "helix", lines/columns start at 1, run-in-terminal supported, memory-references/invalidated-event NOT supported) and stores the server's returned DebuggerCapabilities.

Runtime-dir priority is a fixed 5-tier order highest->lowest: sibling of CARGO_MANIFEST_DIR, <config_dir>/runtime, $HELIX_RUNTIME, build-time HELIX_DEFAULT_RUNTIME, <exe_dir>/runtime; at least two paths are always returned.

merge_toml_values returns a NEW merged toml::Value (value-semantics; consumes left+right), merging arrays by matching the name key and recursing only while merge_depth > 0; default merge depth for languages config is 3.
find_workspace walks CWD ancestors and returns the first dir containing .git/.svn/.jj/.helix, else (CWD, true) where the boolean flags no real workspace found.

Grammar compilation: tree-sitter parser.c (+ optional scanner.c/.cc) is compiled to a shared lib via the cc crate (opt_level 3, C at -std=c11, C++ at c++14, -fno-exceptions, relro,now hardening on Linux/BSD); recompile is gated by mtime comparison.
Version string VERSION_AND_GIT_HASH is a build-time env baked by build.rs from CalVer plus the first 8 chars of git rev-parse HEAD, exposed as a public const.

helix-parsec core contract: ParseResult<'a, Output> = Result<(&'a str, Output), &'a str> threads (remaining_input, value) on success and the input slice on failure; parsers do not mutate input in place; a non_empty guard prevents infinite loops on zero-width matches.

helix-parsec blanket impl: any Fn(&'a str) -> ParseResult<'a, T> automatically IS a Parser, so most combinators are higher-order functions returning closures; Parser is implemented for tuples of arity 2..=10.
helix-stdx Range<T> is half-open [start, end); is_subset/is_exact_subset run in O(m+n) and REQUIRE the caller to uphold that ranges are non-overlapping and sorted.
helix-stdx path transformers have value semantics: normalize/canonicalize return owned PathBuf, and fold_home_dir/expand_tilde/get_relative_path return a Cow<Path> unchanged when no rewrite applies.

helix-vcs provider abstraction: callers go only through DiffProviderRegistry (a Vec<DiffProvider> tried in order, first success wins); the default registry always ends with a DiffProvider::None fallback that bail!s, and the Git variant is present only when the git feature is compiled in, so a no-git build still compiles.
Render-loop isolation: DiffHandle::update_document is only safe to call from the rendering loop; the expensive diff is kept off the async executor via tokio::task::block_in_place.

Hunk list ordering invariant: a Diff is a list of changes sorted in ascending non-overlapping order, enabling binary_search_by_key navigation (next_hunk/prev_hunk/hunk_at); invert is value-cheap/lazy (flips a bool, roles swapped at read time).

helix-vcs diff size guard: diffs are skipped when the file exceeds MAX_DIFF_LINES = 64 * u16::MAX lines OR MAX_DIFF_BYTES = MAX_DIFF_LINES * 128 bytes; over the limit interned_lines() returns None and the diff is not computed.
helix-vcs debounce/render-lock timing: synchronous debounce DIFF_DEBOUNCE_TIME_SYNC = 1ms, async debounce DIFF_DEBOUNCE_TIME_ASYNC = 96ms, rendering blocked at most SYNC_DIFF_TIMEOUT = 12ms.

helix-dap-types serialization contract: every wire struct sets #[serde(rename_all = "camelCase")] mapping Rust snake_case to protocol camelCase, with explicit rename exceptions (clientID/adapterID/linesStartAt1/columnsStartAt1) and the reserved word `type` mapped to a field named `ty`.

helix-event registry uniqueness invariant: an event ID (its type name) may be registered only once; a duplicate registration panics to preserve soundness; status::setup() must be called exactly once during startup before any status message is used (a second call panics).

runtime_local! macro: globals are plain statics in normal builds, but under the integration_test cargo feature they become per-tokio-runtime locals (keyed by tokio::runtime::Id) so multiple helix apps can run in parallel in one process.

beyond_doc.note: resolver "2" workspace of 14 lib/runtime crates + a dev-only xtask member. Grounded @Cargo.toml:1-22 (members list = 14 + xtask). No RC describes the workspace structure / member count.
VCS integration crate (git base version, status, debounced background line-diff via gix). Grounded @helix-vcs/src/lib.rs:1-3, diff.rs:31-44. The RC list has no vcs crate at all.

term imports Rope/Selection/Transaction/Syntax from core. Grounded @helix-term/src/commands.rs:22-46. Doc only states view->core (RC-018) and term->view (RC-019); the direct term->core edge is unstated.

SlotMap<LanguageServerId, Arc<Client>> + parallel HashMap<name,Vec<Arc<Client>>> + transport in-flight request table Mutex<HashMap<jsonrpc::Id,...>>. Grounded @helix-lsp/src/lib.rs:580-587. No RC covers any data store internals.
deliberately version-pinned to =0.1.12 to avoid width-definition drift vs terminals. Grounded @helix-core/Cargo.toml:27-32 (the inline comment confirms the rationale verbatim). No RC mentions it.
packed into ONE AtomicU64 (low 32 = generation counter, high 32 = num_running refcount); cancel bumps generation. Grounded @helix-event/src/cancel.rs:24-71. No RC covers event-system internals.

custom lazy RFC3986 String-newtype instead of the url crate, because LSP uses RFC3986 not WHATWG percent-encoding (strict servers like Deno reject unescaped []). Grounded @helix-stdx/src/uri.rs:1-7. Beyond doc scope.

BORDERLINE-but-distinct: term depends on every other runtime crate and none import it; no helix_term ref in helix-view/src (grep-confirmed = 0). Grounded @helix-view/src/editor.rs:56. Adjacent to RC-020 (core does not depend up) but states the opposite end of the layering, a genuinely separate fact -> NEUTRAL beyond-doc.

scoring_provenance.method: isolated-agent scoring (separation of duties): a key-reader + an EC-extractor (BLIND to the key) + one matcher per RC + a faithfulness refuter per overlap (vs CODE) + a beyond-doc finder. Workflow  ( post-fix v2).
scoring_provenance.orchestrator_caveat: The orchestrating session authored the curator keys, so it is not a fully fresh scorer; but every EC-extraction, match, and faithfulness JUDGMENT was made by an isolated agent (the EC-extractor blind to the key). Recorded honestly.

caveats: INDICATIVE — small-n, fuzzy truth, below any ship-gate
attribution-adjudicated — divergences resolved to CODE, not auto-scored
doc-bounded — agreement with a good human doc, not absolute completeness
training-contamination — helix is a public repo; the blind output may be informed by pretraining, so the number is INDICATIVE.
